title: Threshold comparator to a GPIO
prompt: I want my STM32F103 to get a clean digital 'over threshold' signal when an analog sensor crosses a set point. Use an op-amp (LM358) as a comparator against a reference and route its digital-swinging output to a GPIO input the firmware watches. For the bench test the sensor is above the threshold so the comparator output is high (near the rail).

abstraction.reason: the LM358 comparator has no simulatable SPICE subcircuit; its logic-level OUTPUT is modelled with a #105 voltage_source stand-in at the tested (over-threshold) state
